title: Expressing support for the designation of May 2025 as "National Physical Fitness and Sports Month".
plain_summary: This bill supports making May 2025 a special month to celebrate physical fitness and sports. The bill recognizes that many Americans, especially children and minority groups, have serious weight problems because they eat poorly and do not exercise enough. It explains that being overweight can cause health problems, but being active and playing sports can help people stay healthy. The goal is to encourage more Americans to exercise and participate in sports to improve their health.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution expresses support for making May two thousand twenty-five National Physical Fitness and Sports Month.

sections.title: Findings About Health and Fitness
sections.text: The House of Representatives recognizes several important facts about health in America. Almost forty-two percent of people in the United States were obese from two thousand seventeen through March two thousand twenty. Black adults had the highest rate of obesity at almost fifty percent, followed by Hispanic adults at over forty-five percent, White adults at over forty-one percent, and Asian adults at sixteen percent. Among children and teenagers aged two to nineteen years old, almost twenty percent were obese during two thousand seventeen through two thousand twenty. This affected about fourteen million seven hundred thousand young people. Hispanic children had the highest obesity rate at over twenty-six percent, followed by Black children at almost twenty-five percent, White children at over sixteen percent, and Asian children at nine percent. Research shows that children who are obese are more likely to become obese adults and have physical and mental health problems throughout their lives. Poor eating habits and not getting enough physical activity have caused the obesity problem in America. A bad diet can lead to major health risks that can make people sick or even cause death. Health problems related to obesity include high blood pressure, high cholesterol, type two diabetes, breathing problems like asthma and sleep apnea, and joint problems. Along with eating healthy foods, people in the United States can improve their health and have a better future by being physically active every day. This means thirty minutes of activity for adults and sixty minutes for children. Physical activity can help reduce the risk of long-term health problems like heart disease, many types of cancer, type two diabetes, depression and anxiety, and memory loss diseases. Being physically active can improve brain health, help people maintain a healthy weight, make bones and muscles stronger, and help people sleep better.

sections.title: House Support
sections.text: The House of Representatives supports making May two thousand twenty-five National Physical Fitness and Sports Month. The House also supports efforts to teach people in the United States about the importance of living a healthy lifestyle with good nutrition.
